name: Elote (Mexican Street Corn)
description: Grilled corn on the cob slathered in a creamy mayo-crema mixture and rolled in crumbled cotija, chili powder, cilantro, and lime. A smoky-sweet-tangy summer cookout star. From Hey Grill Hey (your 4-star pick). Standard sizing for ~8 ears.
recipeIngredient: 1 cup cotija cheese, crumbled (plus more for serving)
recipeInstructions: Heat the grill to medium-high. Grill the corn, turning occasionally, until tender and charred in spots all over, about 10-12 minutes.
Meanwhile, stir together the mayonnaise, crema, and garlic.
Slather the grilled corn all over with the mayo-crema mixture.
Sprinkle generously with crumbled cotija, chili powder, and cilantro. Serve with lime wedges to squeeze over.
author: Hey Grill Hey

ai.cliffNotes: Charred grilled corn slathered in mayo-crema, rolled in cotija, chili, cilantro, and lime. Smoky, tangy, and messy in the best way.
keyTips: The char is the point - grill the corn until it has real dark spots; that smoky bitterness balances the rich, tangy crema.
No cotija? Crumbled feta is the standard swap and melts in nicely; both bring the salty-funky note.
Feeding a crowd on plates? Cut the kernels off after grilling and toss everything together as esquites (street-corn salad) - far less messy to serve than on the cob.
Dress the corn while it's hot so the mixture clings, and hit it with the lime right before serving to keep it bright.
